name: 电压
subject: physics
curriculum_points: 一级主题“运动和相互作用”包含“多种多样的运动形式”“机械运动和力”“声和光”“电和磁”四个二级主题。
“电和磁”是“运动和相互作用”主题下的二级主题之一，旨在引导学生了解电和磁的含义，初步形成运动和相互作用观念。

textbook_content.curriculum_standard: 义务教育课程标准（2022年版）
textbook_content.subject: physics
textbook_content.textbook_alignment: 与统编物理课程标准及课标内容要求一致

deep_snippets.text:
In a circuit, if the resistance is left constant and the voltage is doubled (for example, from to ), how does the current change? Does this conform to Ohm’s law? a. The current will get doubled. This conforms to Ohm’s law as the current is proportional to the voltage. b. The current will double. This does not conform to Ohm’s law as the current is proportional to the voltage. 19.1 • Ohm's law
Resistance of a Headlight What is the resistance of an automobile headlight through which 2.50 A flows when 12.0 V is applied to it?
Ohm’s law tells us . The voltage drop in going through the headlight is just the voltage rise supplied by the battery, . We can use this equation and rearrange Ohm’s law to find the resistance of the headlight. Solution Solving Ohm’s law for the resistance of the headlight gives Discussion This is a relatively small resistance. As we will see below, resistances in circuits are commonly measured in kW or MW.
Determine Resistance from Current-Voltage Graph Suppose you apply several different voltages across a circuit and measure the current that runs through the circuit. A plot of your results is shown in Figure 19.7. What is the resistance of the circuit? c. The current will increase by half. This conforms to Ohm’s law as the current is proportional to the voltage. d. The current will decrease by half. This does not conform to Ohm’s law as the current is proportional to the voltage. 19.6
> **[Figure 374, Page 624]** A figure or diagram (302×286px) related to: WORKED, EXAMPLE, Resistance, Headlight, resistance, automobile. This image appears on page 624 of

Figure 19.7 The line shows the current as a function of voltage. Notice that the current is given in milliamperes. For example, at 3 V, the current is 0.003 A, or 3 mA.
The plot shows that current is proportional to voltage, which is Ohm’s law. In Ohm’s law ( ), the constant of proportionality is the resistance R. Because the graph shows current as a function of voltage, we have to rearrange Ohm’s law in that form: . This shows that the slope of the line of I versus V is . Thus, if we find the slope of the line in Figure 19.7, we can calculate the resistance R. Solution The slope of the line is the rise divided by the run. Looking at the lower-left square of the grid, we see that the line rises by 1 mA (0.001 A) and runs over a voltage of 1 V. Thus, the slope of the line is Equating the slope with and solving for R gives or 1 k-ohm. Discussion This resistance is greater than what we found in the previous example. Resistances such as this are common in electric circuits, as we will discover in the next section. Note that if the line in Figure 19.7 were not straight, then the material would not be ohmic and we would not be able to use Ohm’s law. Materials that do not follow Ohm’s law are called nonohmic. Practice Problems 3. If you double the voltage across an ohmic resistor, how does the current through the resistor change? a. The current will double. b. The current will increase by half. c. The current will decrease by half. d. The current will decrease by a factor of two. 4. The current through a resistor is . What is the voltage drop across the resistor? a. b. c. d. 19.7

Figure 19.11 The voltage in the red wire is constant at from the positive terminal of the battery to the top of the resistor. The voltage in the blue wire is constant at from the bottom of the resistor to the negative terminal of the battery. If we go from the blue wire through the battery to the red wire, the voltage increases from to . Likewise, if we go from the blue wire up through the resistor to the red wire, the voltage also goes from to . Thus, using Ohm’s law, we can write Note that is measured from the bottom of the resistor to the top, meaning that the top of the resistor is at a higher voltage than the bottom of the resistor. Thus, current flows from the top of the resistor or higher voltage to the bottom of the resistor or lower voltage. Other possible circuit elements include capacitors and switches. These are drawn as shown on the left side of Figure 19.12. A switch is a device that opens and closes the circuit, like a light switch. It is analogous to a valve in a water circuit, as shown on the right side of Figure 19.12. With the switch open, no current passes through the circuit. With the switch closed, it becomes part of the wire, so the current passes through it with no loss of voltage. The capacitor is labeled C on the left of Figure 19.12. A capacitor in an electrical circuit is analogous to a flexible membrane in a Virtual Physics Battery-Resistor Circuit Click to view content (http://www.openstax.org/l/21batteryresist) Use this simulation to better understand how resistance, voltage, and current are related. With the voltage slider, give the battery a positive voltage. Notice that the electrons are spaced farther apart in the left wire than they are in the right wire. How does this reflect the voltage in the two wires? a. The voltage between static charges is directly proportional to the distance between them. b. The voltage between static charges is directly proportional to square of the distance between them. c. The voltage between static charges is inversely proportional to the distance between them. d. The voltage between static charges is inversely proportional to square of the distance between them. 19.2 • Series Circuits

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 电压

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: middle
- **年级**: 9
- **章节/领域**: 电现象与电路
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 一级主题“运动和相互作用”包含“多种多样的运动形式”“机械运动和力”“声和光”“电和磁”四个二级主题。
> “电和磁”是“运动和相互作用”主题下的二级主题之一，旨在引导学生了解电和磁的含义，初步形成运动和相互作用观念。

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「电压」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「电压」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「电压」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「电压」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“一级主题“运动和相互作用”包含“多种多样的运动形式”“机械运动和力”“声和光”“电和磁”四个二级主题。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「电压」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 围绕课标要点“一级主题“运动和相互作用”包含“多种多样的运动形式”“机械运动和力”“声和光”“电和磁”四个二级主题。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「电压」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“一级主题“运动和相互作用”包含“多种多样的运动形式”“机械运动和力”“声和光”“电和磁”四个二级主题。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系工程、能源、交通或日常生活中的相关现象，讨论「电压」的应用边界。
- 补充科学史或技术应用案例，帮助学生理解物理规律如何服务真实问题。

errors.description: 只套用「电压」相关公式，忽略物理情境、单位和适用条件。
实验分析时没有控制变量，导致无法判断因果关系。

exercises.stem: 根据教材片段，设计一道关于「电压」的现象解释或实验探究题。
exercises.answer: 先描述现象或实验条件，再指出关键概念和证据，最后给出解释或结论。

exercises.stem: 围绕「电压」，写出一个可观察现象，并说明其中涉及的物理量或规律。
exercises.answer: 应扣住课标要点：一级主题“运动和相互作用”包含“多种多样的运动形式”“机械运动和力”“声和光”“电和磁”四个二级主题。，说明现象、条件和规律。

exercises.stem: 如果要验证「电压」相关规律，你会控制哪些变量？记录哪些数据？
exercises.answer: 需说明实验目的、控制变量、测量数据、可能误差和结论依据。

real_world: 观察家用电器、交通工具、运动器材或自然现象中与「电压」相关的物理规律。

excerpts.text: 一级主题“运动和相互作用”包含“多种多样的运动形式”“机械运动和力”“声和光”“电和磁”四个二级主题。
“电和磁”是“运动和相互作用”主题下的二级主题之一，旨在引导学生了解电和磁的含义，初步形成运动和相互作用观念。
